name: Hispanic Real Estate Brokers Association
description_short: HREBA is a HUD-approved 501(c)(3) nonprofit offering free housing counseling, homebuyer workshops, and financial education to low- and moderate-income families in Arlington, TX.
description_long:
The Hispanic Real Estate Brokers Association, Inc. (HREBA) is a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization based in Arlington, Texas, dedicated to expanding housing stability and homeownership opportunities for underserved communities. The organization was founded with a mission to equip individuals and families with education, counseling, and tools needed to build financial resilience and achieve safe, stable housing. HREBA operates under the model of community-based financial empowerment, specifically targeting low- and moderate-income (LMI) residents who face barriers to homeownership and financial stability.

HREBA's core services include HUD-approved housing counseling available in both English and Spanish, homebuyer preparation workshops, financial capability training, and community events focused on homeownership education. The organization provides one-on-one counseling appointments with housing counselors, conducts group workshops on homebuying fundamentals, and hosts annual events like the Homeownership, Health & Wellness Fair. Their counseling services are designed to help clients understand mortgage readiness, credit improvement, and the path to sustainable homeownership, as evidenced by their published success stories.

What distinguishes HREBA is its explicit focus on Hispanic and Latino communities and its bilingual service model, offering counseling and materials in Spanish ("Has Tu Cita, si Hablamos Espanol"). The organization emphasizes community partnerships and volunteer engagement, positioning itself not just as a service provider but as a grassroots empowerment organization. Their approach combines one-on-one counseling with group educational workshops, and they actively document client success stories to demonstrate impact.

HREBA appears legitimate and mission-driven, with clear nonprofit status and housing counseling credentials. However, the website does not display specific counselor certifications, detailed program curricula, or transparent metrics (impact counters on the site show zero values). Prospective clients should contact directly to verify current counselor availability, specific program offerings, and whether services extend beyond the Arlington area. The organization is well-suited for first-time homebuyers seeking free, culturally responsive guidance.
pros: Free HUD-approved housing counseling services with no upfront fees
Bilingual counseling available in English and Spanish
One-on-one appointment scheduling with dedicated housing counselors
Free homebuyer workshops and financial education programs
Documented success stories showing families achieving homeownership goals
Registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit with transparent mission focused on LMI communities
Community events and volunteer opportunities for civic engagement
cons: Website does not list individual counselor certifications or specific HUD approval details
Impact metrics displayed on website show zero values, limiting transparency on actual reach
Limited information about geographic service area (Arlington-based, but unclear if serving broader region)
No published program timeline, curriculum details, or expected counseling duration
Website content suggests organization is small; availability and response times not specified
best_for: First-time homebuyers from Hispanic/Latino communities seeking culturally relevant counseling
Low- to moderate-income families needing free homebuyer education and financial guidance
Spanish-speaking individuals uncomfortable with English-only financial counseling services
Arlington, TX residents looking for local nonprofit housing support and community connections
diagnosis: HREBA is best for first-time homebuyers and low-income families seeking free, bilingual housing counseling and homebuyer education in the Arlington, TX area. The main caveat is that the website provides limited transparency on counselor qualifications, current availability, and service scope, so prospective clients should contact directly to confirm current program capacity and eligibility.
services: Bilingual counseling in Spanish and English
Homebuyer success coaching and goal planning
Community events and fundraisers for homeownership awareness
Volunteer programs and community engagement opportunities
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 410 W Abram St, Suite 112, Arlington, TX 76010
